reviews.review_msg: Maybe he isn't good at teaching lecture courses, but I thought he was a fine lab professor. He talked too much so it was hard to finish labs in time, but he adapted and pre-recorded his pre-lab talk. Explains things clearly. I hated this course so much because it seems like a waste of 4 hours each week, but he wasn't the reason the course sucked!

Even though this lab course was only a 1 unit course, it felt like 3 units at times. Alberding seems like a really nice guy, but somewhat clueless when it came to his students and teaching. The instructions on the lab were very unclear, leading to a lot of extra time and effort deciphering what was required, with little clarification from him.

Jeff is misunderstood. He seems to not care about teaching, but you can't expect much from a 1 credit lab. He is monotone, boring and always looks upset but really he is kind. caring, and very helpful if you speak to him. He lets strudents stay overtime in the lab and is always making sure everybody is on the right track. Grading is fair.

He's kind of intimidating; I felt a bit judged when our group did something wrong sometimes. Honestly, the TAs are more helpful than him during the labs. However, with using the software he was helpful and sometimes will hint answers. I would only take his course for PHYS132 since it is an easier course.

For a physics lab, there isn't much experimentation. Half your grade is based on coding. He yaps a lot before you start the lab and doesn't say much when he yaps. He's a nice person but the labs are very boring and sometimes don't follow the course content that you learn in physics class.

have Dodge for my lab rn and hes really nice and likes to be thorough. Always encourages questions and wants everyone to succeed. he does talk a lot tho sometimes which i feel cuts into our work time, but i would take another course with him nonetheless.

I actually hate this lab so much. He talks for so long, while not saying anything of much importance. And when it is, it's wrong and he has to go back and fix the files he posted. The entire class is coding and uncertainties, very little experimentation. I wish I didn't have to take this class. He is nice though, just an insane yapper.
